findings.claim: The choice of 18 months of data is assumed sufficient to capture evolving attack tactics and concept drift.
findings.failure_mechanism: The duration might not be enough to capture all relevant variations in attack patterns or organizational changes.
findings.evidence_test: Comparing performance on data from additional time periods beyond 18 months.

findings.claim: The use of a standard BERT tokenizer is assumed adequate for tokenizing email body text.
findings.failure_mechanism: Specialized tokenization might be needed for certain types of email content (e.g., heavily encoded or non-standard character sets).
findings.evidence_test: Evaluating tokenizer performance on diverse email content samples.

findings.claim: The metadata features selected are comprehensive enough to capture relevant information for phishing detection.
findings.failure_mechanism: Other metadata features (e.g., email header analysis, more detailed URL analysis) might provide additional signal.
findings.evidence_test: Feature importance analysis or ablation studies to identify critical features.

critic_r1_output.summary: The experiment design is robust and addresses key potential confounds. No material flaws were identified.

adjudicator_output.case_rationale: no advancing findings from critic R1
